LanguageData.Rpt_CantSign_NotTarget: This faction cannot be dealt with diplomatically.
LanguageData.Rpt_Set_DefenseAidFactorTip: Adjusts the size of defense pact reinforcements relative to raid points.
LanguageData.Rpt_Set_TradeFreqFactorTip: Adjusts how often guaranteed caravans from trade agreement partners arrive. Higher = more often.
LanguageData.Rpt_Set_GoodsFeeTip: Price premium applied when buying your ordered goods from the caravan.
LanguageData.Rpt_Set_EdDetected: Economics & Demography detected. The price systems overlap, so faction market prices are disabled automatically.
LanguageData.Rpt_Set_GoodsCapTip: Maximum estimated total value (market price × premium) of a single goods order. Factions with trust 80 or higher allow orders up to twice this value.
LanguageData.Rpt_Set_TributeIncomeFactorTip: Multiplier applied to the quarterly silver income received from your tributaries.
LanguageData.Rpt_Set_ApfpDetected: A Petition for Provisions detected. The goods request features overlap, so RimPacts goods ordering is disabled automatically.
LanguageData.Rpt_Set_MarketPricesTip: Prices vary by goods category depending on each faction's tech level and character.
LanguageData.Rpt_Set_RouteRiskTip: Adjusts how often trade routes with warring factions lose their quarterly income.
LanguageData.Rpt_Set_VteDetected: Vanilla Trading Expanded detected — faction market prices are automatically disabled to avoid conflicting price systems.
LanguageData.Rpt_CantSign_Empire: The Empire does not sign this kind of treaty.
LanguageData.Rpt_CantSign_AlreadyActive: This treaty is already in effect.
LanguageData.Rpt_CantSign_Hostile: Cannot sign treaties with a hostile faction.
LanguageData.Rpt_LetterTreatyExpireSoon: The treaty ({0}) with {1} expires in about {2} days.\n\nYou can renew it from the diplomacy tab.
LanguageData.Rpt_LetterTreatyExpired: The treaty ({0}) with {1} has expired. Honoring the contract to the end has deepened their trust in you.
LanguageData.Rpt_LetterTreatyDissolved: Relations with {1} have collapsed, and the treaty ({0}) is no longer in effect.
LanguageData.Rpt_CantSign_TributeCooldown: Your tribute demand was recently refused. (can demand again in {0} days)
LanguageData.Rpt_CantSign_TributeConflict: A tribute in the opposite direction is already active with this faction.
LanguageData.Rpt_CantSign_TributeGoodwill: Relations are too poor — they refuse to accept tribute.
LanguageData.Rpt_CantSign_DefenseConflict: A defense pact cannot coexist with a tribute arrangement.
LanguageData.Rpt_CantSign_Orbital: This treaty cannot be signed with an orbital faction.
LanguageData.Rpt_LetterTributePayWarn: You could not afford the tribute ({1} silver) owed to {0}. They will let it slide until next quarter — miss it again and everything collapses.
LanguageData.Rpt_LetterTributeFailed: You failed to pay tribute ({1} silver) to {0} two quarters in a row. The tributary relation and the non-aggression pact have been torn up, and harsh retaliation is coming.
LanguageData.Rpt_MsgTributeAccepted: {0} has submitted to your tribute demand. They will send silver every quarter.
LanguageData.Rpt_MsgTributeRefused: {0} refused your tribute demand. Relations have soured.
LanguageData.Rpt_LetterTributeRevolt: Seeing your weakened forces, {0} has thrown off the tributary pact. Expect retaliation.
LanguageData.Rpt_LetterDefenseAid: {0} has dispatched reinforcements under your defense pact. They are arriving now.
LanguageData.Rpt_LetterTreatyOffer: {0} has offered to sign a treaty ({1}). Since they proposed it, accepting costs nothing.
LanguageData.Rpt_LetterDefenseCall: {0}, your defense pact partner, is in trouble and asks for support. Sending {1} silver will deepen their trust; repeated refusals will strain the pact.
LanguageData.Rpt_LetterDefenseTerminated: Disappointed by repeated refusals, {0} has torn up the defense pact. Having lost faith in the alliance, they drop relations to neutral.
LanguageData.Rpt_Chat_MsgBreak: I notify {0} that I am ending the {1}.
LanguageData.Rpt_Chat_MsgResign: I capitulate to {0} and offer to become a tributary once more.
LanguageData.Rpt_Chat_MsgEmbargo: I declare a trade embargo against {0}.
LanguageData.Rpt_Chat_MsgCoalition: I move to form a coalition against {0}.
LanguageData.Rpt_Chat_MsgAssassinate: I put out a contract on {0}'s leader.
LanguageData.Rpt_Chat_MsgIndependence: I back a breakaway movement within {0}.
LanguageData.Rpt_Chat_MsgGuaranteeEnd: I withdraw my guarantee of {0}'s independence.
LanguageData.Rpt_UI_DemandConfirm: Demand tribute from {0}?\n\nCurrent power advantage x{1}, submission {2}. If they submit, they will send {3} silver every quarter. If refused, relations suffer badly and you cannot demand again for a while.
LanguageData.Rpt_UI_DemandConfirmFail: {0} will not submit yet.\n\nPower advantage x{1} (needs x{2}), submission {3} (needs {4}). Raise submission further by destroying their settlements, embargo, and so on. Demanding now will be refused, badly hurting relations and blocking further demands for a while. Continue anyway?
LanguageData.Rpt_UI_SubmitHintWeak: Lacking outside protection, they yield more easily to economic and diplomatic pressure.
LanguageData.Rpt_UI_SubmitHintStrong: Shielded by outside allies, they will not yield to economic pressure alone — military pressure is needed.
LanguageData.Rpt_UI_EmpireGate: The Empire only allows tribute demands once its power is broken below {0}. Destroy its settlements to lower its power (−6 each).
LanguageData.Rpt_Act_EmpireDeclareWeak: Not yet strong enough to challenge the Empire (needs power advantage x{0})
LanguageData.Rpt_Act_EmpireDeclareConfirm: Demand submission from {0}?\n\nThis is a declaration of war. You will become a PERMANENT ENEMY of the Empire, and it cannot be undone. You must destroy its settlements to break its power and force submission, and it will raid you meanwhile. If you succeed, the permanent hostility is lifted and it becomes your tributary. Proceed?
LanguageData.Rpt_LetterEmpireWar: You have declared war demanding {0}'s submission. You are now a permanent enemy of the Empire. Destroy its settlements to break its power and force submission. Succeed, and the hostility lifts and it becomes your tributary.
LanguageData.Rpt_LetterEmpireSubjugated: {0} has finally bent the knee. The permanent hostility is lifted and the Empire is now your tributary, paying tribute each quarter.
LanguageData.Rpt_UI_ReleaseConfirm: Release {0} from tribute? They will be grateful, and relations will recover somewhat.
LanguageData.Rpt_UI_BreakConfirm: Break the treaty ({0}) with {1}?\n\nTrust {2} and goodwill {3} will be applied, and other factions' trust will drop by {4} (word spreads).
LanguageData.Rpt_GoodsRequest_AlreadyOrdered: An order is already in progress.
LanguageData.Rpt_GoodsRequest_Cooldown: They handled your last order too recently. (can order again in {0} days)
LanguageData.Rpt_GR_SelectItem: Select items from the list on the left. You can order several at once.
LanguageData.Rpt_GR_MaxLinesReached: A single order can contain at most {0} different items.
LanguageData.Rpt_GR_PayNote: No prepayment. Your order will appear highlighted in the arriving caravan's sale stock, at a premium price for the special request.
LanguageData.Rpt_LetterGoodsArrival: A caravan from {0} has arrived carrying your order ({1}). It is highlighted in their sale stock — buy it before the caravan leaves.
LanguageData.Rpt_LetterGoodsArrivalOrbital: An orbital trade ship from {0} has arrived carrying your order ({1}). Use a comms console (with a trade beacon) to buy it before the ship departs.
LanguageData.Rpt_MsgGoodsNotBought: The caravan from {0} left, taking the unpurchased order ({1}) with them.
LanguageData.Rpt_TradeOrderedTip: Goods you ordered. Sold at a premium as a special request.
LanguageData.Rpt_UI_OrderLine: Order en route: {0} — arriving in ~{1} days
LanguageData.Rpt_UI_OrderArrivedLine: Order arrived: {0} — buy before the caravan leaves
LanguageData.Rpt_LetterGoodsCancelled: Relations with {0} have collapsed and your order was cancelled.
LanguageData.Rpt_SurrenderOffer: The colony is breaking. Pay {1} silver to {0} and become their tributary, and the raiders will withdraw.\n\nSurrender? You cannot end the tribute for {2} days, and word of your humiliation will spread across the rim. Refusing costs nothing.
LanguageData.Rpt_LetterSurrender: You paid {1} silver to {0} and became their tributary. The raiders are withdrawing. You cannot end the tribute for {2} days, and word of your surrender has spread.
LanguageData.Rpt_UI_TreatyLocked: Locked by surrender terms. (unlocks in {0} days)
LanguageData.Rpt_TT_Orbital: Trade routes cannot be opened to orbital factions.
LanguageData.Rpt_MsgCompanyFounded: Trading company founded. Open trade routes from the Trade tab on world map settlements.
LanguageData.Rpt_TT_CloseConfirm: Close the trade route with {0}? The opening fee is not refunded.
LanguageData.Rpt_TT_RouteInfo: Trade route active — quarterly income ~{0} silver
LanguageData.Rpt_TT_RouteRisk: At war — {0}% chance per quarter to lose the income
LanguageData.Rpt_TT_NoSlots: All route slots are in use. (upgrade the company)
LanguageData.Rpt_MsgRouteIncome: Trade route income: {1} silver arrived from {0}.
LanguageData.Rpt_LetterRouteRaided: The trade route to {0} was caught in the fighting. This quarter's income is lost.
LanguageData.Rpt_LetterRouteClosed: The trade route with {0} has collapsed — the agreement broke, relations soured, or the settlement is gone.
LanguageData.Rpt_LetterAidLapsed: You could not afford the aid promised to {0}. They are disappointed.
LanguageData.Rpt_LetterRelief: {0} has been devastated by {1}. Sending relief supplies would be long remembered.
LanguageData.Rpt_MsgReliefSent: Sent relief supplies to {0}. A grateful caravan will visit soon.
LanguageData.Rpt_Act_InsultConfirm: Publicly insult {0}?\n\nRelations will suffer badly, and they may furiously tear up their treaties with you first (costing you no reputation). Word of your rudeness slightly lowers other factions' trust.
LanguageData.Rpt_LetterInsultBreak: Enraged by the insult, {0} has torn up all treaties with you.
LanguageData.Rpt_Act_EmbargoConfirm: Impose an embargo on {0}?\n\nYour trade agreement, routes and monopoly with them are torn up immediately (with reputation loss), their caravans and visitors stop coming, and settlement trade is blocked.
LanguageData.Rpt_UI_EmbargoActive: Embargo active — relations worsen each quarter.
LanguageData.Rpt_MsgEmbargoEnd: Lifted the embargo on {0}. A grudge lingers in trade prices for a while.
LanguageData.Rpt_Act_UltConfirm:
Send an ultimatum to {0}?

Demand: {1}
Current power advantage x{2} (accepted at x{3}).

If they submit, you take the demand but earn deep resentment. If refused, relations suffer badly and they may tear up treaties or strike back — but you gain a 'just cause' for 30 days (no reputation loss from acting against them).
LanguageData.Rpt_Act_UltCooldown: You sent an ultimatum recently. ({0} days)
LanguageData.Rpt_MsgUltAccepted: {0} accepted your ultimatum. Deep resentment remains.
LanguageData.Rpt_MsgUltRefused: {0} refused your ultimatum and is enraged. You gained a just cause — but watch for retaliation. ({1} days)
LanguageData.Rpt_TT_NoPath: No path, or too far. (max {0} tiles)
LanguageData.Rpt_LetterRoadDone: The {1} to {0}'s settlement is open. Caravans can travel quickly.
LanguageData.Rpt_Act_IndependenceTribute: Cannot be used on a faction in a tribute relation.
LanguageData.Rpt_Act_IndependenceConfirm: Support the separatists inside {0}?\n\nFor {1} silver and {2} days of scheming, one of their settlements will break away as a new faction — at war with its mother faction, and friendly toward you.
LanguageData.Rpt_MsgIndependenceDetected: {0} has caught wind of our involvement.
LanguageData.Rpt_MsgGuaranteeLapse: {0} has grown strong enough; the guarantee of independence has lapsed.
LanguageData.Rpt_MsgIndependenceFail: The independence scheme inside {0} came to nothing.
LanguageData.Rpt_LetterIndependence: {0} has broken away from {1} and declared independence. They remember your support.
LanguageData.Rpt_MsgCoalitionNone: No faction answered the coalition call.
LanguageData.Rpt_LetterCoalitionDisband: With {0}'s power broken, the coalition has disbanded.
LanguageData.Rpt_Act_GuaranteeNeedWeak: Only weak factions can be guaranteed. (power below {0})
LanguageData.Rpt_MsgGuarantee: You declared a guarantee of {0}'s independence.
LanguageData.Rpt_LetterGuaranteeBroken: You turned on {0}, whose independence you guaranteed. The guarantee is void, and word of your hypocrisy spreads.
LanguageData.Rpt_Act_MissionaryNoLeader: Only a moral guide can be sent as a missionary.
LanguageData.Rpt_MsgMissionarySent: {0} left as a missionary to {1}. ({2} days)
LanguageData.Rpt_LetterConvertOk: {0} has embraced {1}. A shared faith now binds your peoples.
LanguageData.Rpt_LetterConvertFail: {0} rejected the mission. Relations soured somewhat.
LanguageData.Rpt_LetterWar: War has broken out between {0} and {1}. Their markets churn, and trade routes through the fighting are at risk.\n\nWhose side will you take? Backing one draws you closer to them — and makes the other your enemy at once.
LanguageData.Rpt_War_BackGuaranteedWarn: Backing this side betrays your guarantee of {0}
LanguageData.Rpt_MsgWarBacked: You declared support for {0}. {1} is now hostile to you.
